analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet defends an Agreement with China as upholding EU interests and values, giving leverage against forced labour, and engaging China to protect the climate under the Paris Agreement and promote rules-based multilateralism. Evaluation: EU1 (successful story) – Not about EU’s historical success or overcoming past obstacles; not promoted. EU2 (united) – No mention of EU unity or collective agreement; not promoted. EU3 (useful for its citizens) – Focus is external engagement with China, not internal citizen benefits; not promoted. EU4 (avant-garde actor) – It hints at leadership on climate and rules, but the tweet frames engagement and multilateralism rather than EU pioneering new standards; insufficient to claim avant-garde; not promoted. EU5 (global champion of fair causes) – The tweet highlights eradicating forced labour (human rights) and promoting rule-based order, which aligns with defending fair causes globally; promoted. EU6 (contributes to a better world) – Emphasizes engaging China to protect the climate and promote multilateralism, showing EU contribution to global goods; promoted. Conclusion: EU5 and EU6 apply.
